sn2.25:1.2: that on one occasion many monks were staying among the Kosalans in wilderness huts: high-strung, rowdy, unruly, talkative, of loose words & muddled mindfulness, unalert, unconcentrated, their minds scattered, their faculties left wide open.
sn2.25:2.1: Then Jantu the deva’s son, on the uposatha of the fifteenth, went to those monks and, on arrival, addressed them in verses.
sn2.25:3.1: They lived happily in the past,
sn2.25:3.2: the monks who were Gotama’s disciples.
sn2.25:3.3: Without wishes, 		they sought their alms.
sn2.25:3.6: with regard to the world, they made an end of suffering.
sn2.25:4.1: [But these,] making themselves hard to maintain,
sn2.25:5.1: Placing my hands over my heart to the Saṅgha,
